### Overall Assessment
You got *3 out of 15* questions right (20 %). While the result is lower than you hoped for, it’s a clear picture of where the misconceptions lie. Most of the items that “feel” bigger (e.g., Earth vs. Mars, Africa vs. South America) were answered the opposite way, so a quick review of basic world‑geography, basic astronomy, and some common‑sense size comparisons should boost your score dramatically.

### Quick Study Tips
1. *Size vs. mass vs. quantity* – Keep straight whether the question asks about physical size (diameter, height), mass/weight, or count (people, trees, rooms).  
2. *Familiar reference points* –  
   * Earth ≈ 12,700 km diameter, Mars ≈ 6,800 km.  
   * Africa ≈ 30 million km², South America ≈ 18 million km².  
   * Old Testament ≈ 23,000 verses vs. New Testament ≈ 8,000.  
   * French budget ≈ €350 bn vs. Google revenue ≈ €260 bn.  
   * Trees on Earth ≈ 3 trillion vs. people ≈ 8 billion.  
3. *Remember the “obvious” outliers* – The heaviest pumpkin on record (> 1 ton) easily beats the heaviest human; a terabyte is 1 000 times a gigabyte; a division in a modern army is far larger than a regiment.  

With a brief refresher on these facts, you should be able to turn that 20 % into a much higher score next time. Good luck!
